summary: End-to-end testing of PETER AI v4.0 — backend (meta, tiers, agents, router, chat, sessions, crew, stats) and frontend (Home, Chat, Router, Crew, Cost, Settings, Nav) all pass. Crew 7-agent workflow completes successfully in ~2 minutes.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: ai_router.py imports `asyncio` and `time` (line 9, 11) but only `time` is used; unused imports.
Minor: server.py imports `time` but does not use it; cleanup recommended.
Minor: HomeView line 118 expression `(t || []) && t` is redundant — `t &&` is enough.
Suggestion: Consider rate-limiting /api/crew/build to avoid runaway costs since it kicks off 7 sequential LLM calls.
seed_data_creation: Created via test runs: 5+ chat sessions, 1 completed crew run (7 agents done), populated stats endpoint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_peter_ai.py. Full suite passes in ~2:30 (crew test is slow, gated by 7 sequential LLM calls). Skip crew test with `-k 'not eventually_done'` for fast iteration. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is live and working. Tier classification is pure pattern-match (no LLM). Force-tier dropdown in Chat works as expected.

backend_endpoints_tested: GET /api/ → meta returns PETER AI v4.0.0 + tagline ✓
GET /api/tiers → 4 tiers with all required fields ✓
POST /api/chat (auto) → routes to FREE for simple, cost=0, saved>0, latency>0 ✓
GET /api/crew/runs/{id} → polled to status='done', all 7 agents done, total_saved_usd populated ✓
frontend_flows_tested: Home / → PETER AI title, tagline, 4 stat cards, 4 tier cards, both CTAs ✓
Chat /chat → sent 'what time is it in tokyo' → FREE tier badge displayed; switched to SMART → second message shows SMART badge ✓
Router /router → 'analyze our churn strategy' classified to SMART, node-tier-smart visible ✓
Crew /crew → started a build, all 7 agent-node-{id} cards rendered immediately ✓
Cost /cost → all 4 metric tiles, pie chart, bar chart, recent-table visible ✓
Settings /settings → tier catalog cards + docker quick-start snippet ✓
